data.title: European Commission President to Visit Greenland in March
data.description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen is scheduled to visit Greenland in March. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this visit in strengthening ties between the European Union and Greenland. The visit is expected to focus on discussions around economic cooperation and environmental issues. This trip underscores the EU's interest in the Arctic region, particularly in light of its strategic and environmental importance. Further details on the agenda and specific meetings are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: Hedera Faces Bearish Pressure Amid Potential Liquidation Risk
data.description: Hedera (HBAR) is experiencing bearish pressure with a notable number of short futures contracts open, posing a risk of $4.9 million in liquidations if the price exceeds $0.1143. According to NS3.AI, the Chaikin Money Flow indicator is showing initial signs of capital inflows, suggesting a potential short-term recovery. Additionally, HBAR's correlation with Bitcoin has decreased, which may allow it to diverge from broader market trends. Key technical levels to watch include resistance at $0.1035 and support at $0.0961; failure to maintain support could result in further declines.

data.title: CME Predicts Low Probability of Fed Rate Cuts by March
data.description: The CME's FedWatch tool indicates a mere 5.9% chance of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by 25 basis points by March, with a 94.1% likelihood of rates remaining unchanged. According to Jin10, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut by April stands at 20.5%, while the chance of maintaining current rates is 78.5%. Additionally, there is a 1.0% probability of a cumulative 50 basis point cut by April. By June, the likelihood of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut increases to 49.8%.

data.title: Walmart to Announce Earnings Amid Market Watch on Key Stocks
data.description: Walmart is set to release its earnings report tomorrow, drawing significant attention from investors. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X. Alongside Walmart, market watchers are also keeping an eye on the earnings of Live Nation Entertainment, Wayfair, and Newmont Corporation, both before and after the market bell. These reports are anticipated to provide insights into the current economic climate and consumer behavior.

data.title: Tech Stocks Attract Investors After Challenging Start to 2026
data.description: Investors are showing renewed interest in technology stocks following a difficult beginning to 2026. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that the initial downturn transformed some of the most expensive companies in the market into surprising bargains. This shift in investor sentiment highlights the volatility and potential opportunities within the tech sector. As the year progresses, market participants are closely monitoring these developments, assessing the potential for recovery and growth in the technology industry.

data.title: Base Transitions to Unified Software Architecture for Enhanced Efficiency
data.description:
Base, a decentralized Ethereum layer-2 scaling network, announced on Wednesday its transition from utilizing the L2 blockchain protocol Optimism’s tech stack to operating on its own unified software architecture. According to Cointelegraph, this shift aims to reduce reliance on external service providers and expedite the deployment of new upgrades. Launched in 2023 as an Optimism chain, Base is now consolidating its operations to streamline software packaging and release processes. The team stated that each upgrade will feature a single Base binary for node operation, simplifying the network's sequencer, which is crucial for transaction ordering by validators.

The transition will occur in four phases, as outlined in the project's roadmap, requiring node operators to adopt the new Base client over the coming months for official updates. This move comes amid broader discussions in the Ethereum community about the future of layer-2 scaling networks. Earlier this month, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin expressed a shift in perspective regarding the role of L2s in scaling Ethereum. He noted that while L2s are taking longer to achieve full decentralization, the Ethereum L1 is already scaling effectively with low network fees. Buterin suggested that the original vision for L2s needs reevaluation, prompting mixed reactions from L2 teams.

Base founder Jesse Pollak responded positively to Ethereum's L1 scaling, emphasizing that L2s must evolve beyond being merely cost-effective execution layers for Ethereum. Other L2 founders maintain that scaling layers are aligned with Ethereum's long-term objectives. As of the time of publication, there are over 128 different Ethereum L2 scaling networks, according to L2Beat. This development highlights the ongoing evolution and adaptation within the Ethereum ecosystem as it seeks to enhance efficiency and scalability.

data.title: Japan's 20-Year Bond Sale to Gauge Investor Interest Post-Election
data.description: Japan is set to test investor interest in its super-long bonds with the sale of 20-year government bonds on Thursday. Bloomberg posted on X that this marks the first such sale since Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's landslide election victory, which has spurred increased buying activity. The outcome of this bond sale will provide insights into market sentiment and investor confidence following the political shift. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation to assess the potential impact on Japan's financial markets.

data.title: U.S. Forces to Withdraw from Syria Amid Strategic Shift
data.description: The Trump administration is set to withdraw military forces from Syria, marking a strategic shift in the region. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision comes as President Ahmed al-Sharaa's government assumes control over the fight against terror groups. A senior U.S. official confirmed the move, highlighting the changing dynamics in Syria's ongoing conflict.

data.title: New Scorecard Approach for Benchmarking U.S. Private Firms
data.description: Over 1.5 million U.S. private firms generate more than $1 million annually, yet reliable benchmarking remains challenging. FactSet posted on X that a new scorecard approach has been developed to utilize tax-imputed financials for comparing performance across 10,000 sponsor-backed companies. This method provides deep insights through five distinct lenses, aiming to enhance the understanding of financial health and operational efficiency among these firms.

data.title: Affordability Takes Center Stage in Upcoming Midterm Elections
data.description: Affordability is a central issue in the upcoming November midterm elections, with living standards consistently being a critical area of focus. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that despite some positive economic data, the overall sentiment in the U.S. economy remains pessimistic. The economic landscape continues to be a significant factor influencing voter sentiment as the elections approach.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. Justice Department Engages Theater Chains Over Warner Bros. Discovery Sale
data.description: The U.S. Justice Department has initiated discussions with major theater chains regarding the implications of a potential sale of Warner Bros. Discovery. Bloomberg posted on X, citing individuals familiar with the situation, that these private conversations aim to assess the impact on the industry. The move comes amid ongoing speculation about the future of Warner Bros. Discovery, a significant player in the entertainment sector. The Justice Department's involvement underscores the importance of understanding how such a transaction could affect competition and market dynamics within the film distribution landscape. As the situation develops, stakeholders are closely monitoring the discussions for any indications of regulatory actions or changes in the industry.

data.title: Solana bolsters tooling as Phantom debuts MCP server for AI
data.description:
Phantom MCP server lets MCP clients sign transactions and execute cross-chain swaps; devs cite security, compliance, Solana support, and Claude/OpenClaw.
source: https://coincu.com/news/solana-bolsters-tooling-as-phantom-debuts-mcp-server-for-ai/

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Oil Prices Surge Amid Hormuz Strait Closure Concerns
data.description: Oil futures have rebounded from a two-week low, reaching their highest level since late January. According to RTHK, Iranian state media reported that parts of the Hormuz Strait will be temporarily closed due to military exercises, raising concerns about potential disruptions in oil supply. Meanwhile, negotiations between Ukraine and Russia in Geneva have yet to achieve any significant progress. In New York, March crude oil closed at $65.19 per barrel, marking an increase of $2.86 or 4.59%. Brent crude for April delivery settled at $70.35 per barrel, up by $2.93 or 4.35%.

data.title: Currency Hedging by Foreign Investors May Impact Dollar, JPMorgan Strategists Say
data.description: Foreign investors are expected to enhance their hedging strategies against currency risk on their dollar-denominated assets, according to JPMorgan strategists. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move could contribute to the existing pressures on the U.S. dollar. The strategists suggest that the increased hedging activity may lead to further fluctuations in the currency market, as investors seek to mitigate potential losses from exchange rate volatility. This trend reflects growing concerns over the stability of the dollar amid global economic uncertainties. JPMorgan's analysis indicates that the shift in investor behavior could have significant implications for currency markets, potentially influencing the dollar's performance in the near future.

data.title: Federal Reserve Endorses Kalshi's Prediction Market for Economic Insights
data.description: The U.S. Federal Reserve has released a report acknowledging the value of Kalshi's prediction market as a real-time reference for policymakers. According to NS3.AI, the platform's market prices offer detailed probability-based insights into economic outlooks. This endorsement highlights Kalshi's potential as a significant tool for guiding research and policy decisions.

data.title: FTSE Russell Seeks Market Feedback on 'Fast-Track Inclusion' for IPOs
data.description: FTSE Russell is currently soliciting market opinions on its 'fast-track inclusion' rules for initial public offerings (IPOs). According to Jin10, the discussion includes potential IPOs from companies such as SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic. The move aims to streamline the process for incorporating significant IPOs into its indices, reflecting the growing interest in these high-profile companies. The consultation seeks to address how these potential IPOs could impact market dynamics and investor strategies.

data.title: Illinois Governor Proposes Temporary Suspension of Data Center Incentives to Address Rising Power Costs
data.description: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker has proposed a temporary suspension of incentives for data centers as part of efforts to manage increasing power bills. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the governor's initiative aimed at addressing the financial strain caused by escalating energy costs. The move comes amid growing concerns over the impact of high electricity prices on both businesses and consumers in the state. Governor Pritzker's proposal seeks to alleviate the burden by pausing incentives that have been contributing to the rise in power consumption. This decision is part of a broader strategy to ensure sustainable energy practices and financial stability for Illinois residents and businesses. The governor's office is expected to engage with stakeholders to discuss the potential implications and benefits of this temporary measure.
